title: Task Ambiguity in Humans and Language Models

structured.summary: The paper introduces AmbiBench, a benchmark to study task ambiguity in language models and humans by evaluating their performance on ambiguously specified tasks. Results show that large models trained with reinforcement learning from human feedback outperform humans, and finetuning on ambiguous tasks improves model generalization.

strengths.evidence: The paper presents AmbiBench, a newly developed benchmark specifically designed to study task ambiguity in language models.
Models with 175 billion parameters and trained with RLHF were able to match or surpass human participants in task disambiguation.
The paper shows how finetuning on ambiguous in-context examples can improve standard models' performance, leading them to potentially exceed RLHF models on unseen tasks.

weaknesses.evidence: The study uses existing methods like RLHF and model scaling without original methodological contribution (Section 1).
Tasks are artificial and may not reflect real-world ambiguity effectively (Section 3.1).
The benchmark evaluates only six tasks, which may not capture the full breadth of task ambiguity (Section 3).
There are no comparisons to other benchmarks or techniques addressing ambiguity (Section 2.2).
The description of how ambiguity is measured and analyzed is vague (Section 3.2).
Lacks detailed information on experiment settings and hyperparameters (Section 3.3).
Claims models can "approach or exceed the accuracy of human participants" without explicit evidence (Abstract, Section 4).
Focus is on large model performance without exploring efficiency improvements in smaller models (Abstract).
No discussion of qualitative results or specific examples of task ambiguity challenging for models (Section 4).
Does not address task ambiguity in more complex real-world tasks beyond binary sentence classification (Section 3.1).
The exclusive use of synthetic task templates could skew results as they may not capture real-world task nuances (Section 3.2).
The definition and scope of "task ambiguity" could have been more clearly defined, which is crucial for the paper's benchmark and claims (Section 1).

questions.question: How does AmbiBench compare to existing benchmarks addressing task ambiguity?
questions.motivation: Understanding the relative effectiveness of AmbiBench could highlight its contributions or limitations compared to existing benchmarks.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of novelty in approach.. The study uses existing methods like RLHF and model scaling without original methodological contribution (Section 1)
critique_points.point_002: Ambiguity in task construction.. Tasks are artificial and may not reflect real-world ambiguity effectively (Section 3.1)
critique_points.point_003: Limited scope of evaluation.. The benchmark evaluates only six tasks, which may not capture the full breadth of task ambiguity (Section 3)
critique_points.point_004: Inadequate baseline comparisons.. There are no comparisons to other benchmarks or techniques addressing ambiguity (Section 2.2)
critique_points.point_005: Methodological ambiguities.. The description of how ambiguity is measured and analyzed is vague (Section 3.2)
critique_points.point_006: Insufficient detail in reproducibility.. Lacks detailed information on experiment settings and hyperparameters (Section 3.3)
critique_points.point_007: Overestimation of model capabilities.. Claims models can "approach or exceed the accuracy of human participants" without explicit evidence (Abstract, Section 4)
critique_points.point_008: Overemphasis on model size.. Focus is on large model performance without exploring efficiency improvements in smaller models (Abstract)
critique_points.point_009: Lack of contextual insight.. No discussion of qualitative results or specific examples of task ambiguity challenging for models (Section 4)
critique_points.point_010: Restriction on practical utility.. Does not address task ambiguity in more complex real-world tasks beyond binary sentence classification (Section 3.1)
critique_points.point_011: Over-Reliance on Synthetic Data.. The exclusive use of synthetic task templates could skew results as they may not capture real-world task nuances (Section 3.2)
critique_points.point_012: Ambiguity Definition.. The definition and scope of "task ambiguity" could have been more clearly defined, which is crucial for the paper's benchmark and claims (Section 1)
